description: Create the NixOS agent container configuration: packages, home-manager, NixOS module, and flake outputs for aarch64-linux
steps: Step 1: Create nix/packages/agent-container.nix - a function {pkgs}: returning a list of Linux-compatible CLI packages for AI agent execution. Include: bashInteractive, coreutils, findutils, gnugrep, gnused, gawk, curl, wget, cacert, unzip, zstd, tree, git, gh, jq, yq-go, ripgrep, fd, fzf, bat, eza, tmux, neovim, direnv, nix-direnv, just, gnumake, pkg-config, cmake, nodejs_24, pnpm, python313, uv, rustup, awscli2, kubectl, alejandra, deadnix, statix, nixd, shellcheck, shfmt, codex, claude-code. Do NOT include macism, zed-editor, kiroCliFixed, browserUse, GUI tools, or anything depending on /Users/adachi.

Step 2: Create nix/home-agent-container.nix - Home Manager configuration for the container user. Accept username and homeDirectory as arguments (via the module system or specialArgs). Configure: programs.home-manager.enable, programs.bash.enable with shell aliases (ca, cc, cdx), programs.git (same settings as home.nix but without credential helper depending on macOS), programs.direnv + nix-direnv, programs.fzf with bash integration, programs.tmux (vi mode, mouse, clock24). Set xdg.configFile."nvim" to source ../home/config/nvim recursively. Set home.sessionVariables EDITOR=nvim. Set home.sessionPath to ["$HOME/bin" "$HOME/.local/bin"]. Place agent config files: home.file.".agents/AGENTS.md".source = ../home/ai/AGENTS.md; home.file.".codex/AGENTS.md".source = ../home/ai/AGENTS.md; home.file.".codex/config.toml" (generate a minimal container-suitable version without /Users/adachi paths, macOS plugins, or marketplace references); home.file.".codex/openai.config.toml".source = ../home/agents/codex/openai.config.toml; home.file.".codex/bedrock.config.toml" (generate a minimal version without /Users/adachi paths); home.file.".codex/keybindings.json".source = ../home/agents/codex/keybindings.json; home.file.".codex/rules/default.rules".source = ../home/agents/codex/default.rules; home.file.".codex/notify.sh" (generate a simplified version that just prints to stdout since no macOS/Windows notification available); home.file.".claude/AGENTS.md".source = ../home/ai/AGENTS.md; home.file.".claude/CLAUDE.md".source = ../home/ai/CLAUDE.md. Do NOT import agent-skills-nix or nix/agents.nix. Do NOT use mutableHomeFile or depend on /Users/adachi/.config/nix-darwin. stateVersion = "25.05".

Step 3: Create nix/nixos/agent-container.nix - NixOS module with minimal system configuration. Set system.stateVersion = "25.05". Enable nix with experimental-features = ["nix-command" "flakes"]. Set nixpkgs.config.allowUnfree = true. Create users.users.adachi with isNormalUser=true, shell=pkgs.bashInteractive, extraGroups=["wheel"], home="/home/adachi". Enable passwordless sudo for wheel group (security.sudo.wheelNeedsPassword = false). Set networking.hostName = "agent-container". Disable firewall (networking.firewall.enable = false). Do NOT enable openssh by default but add a commented note about how to enable it. Install the agent-container packages as environment.systemPackages. Import home-manager NixOS module and configure home-manager.users.adachi to use nix/home-agent-container.nix with appropriate specialArgs (username="adachi", homeDirectory="/home/adachi").

Step 4: Modify flake.nix to add Linux outputs while preserving the existing darwinConfigurations.macbook. Add a let binding for linuxSystem = "aarch64-linux" and linuxPkgs = import nixpkgs { system = linuxSystem; config.allowUnfree = true; }. Add outputs: packages.aarch64-linux.agent-container-tools (a symlinkJoin or buildEnv wrapping the packages from nix/packages/agent-container.nix); devShells.aarch64-linux.agent-container (a mkShell with the same packages plus any dev niceties); nixosConfigurations.agent-container-aarch64-linux (using nixpkgs.lib.nixosSystem with system=linuxSystem, importing nix/nixos/agent-container.nix and home-manager.nixosModules.home-manager). CRITICAL: The Linux outputs must NOT reference the agent-skills or agent-skills-nix inputs. The existing darwin config can keep using them. Structure the outputs so that the agent-skills input is only passed to darwin modules.

Step 5: Update README.md to add a '## NixOS Agent Container' section after the existing 'Daily Workflow' section. Document: purpose (isolated Linux environment for AI agents on Apple Silicon), architecture (aarch64-linux NixOS, no macOS dependencies), secrets policy (not included, mount at runtime), relationship to macOS profile (separate, independent), verification commands (nix flake check, nix build .#packages.aarch64-linux.agent-container-tools, nix develop .#agent-container, nix build .#nixosConfigurations.agent-container-aarch64-linux.config.system.build.toplevel), Home Manager standalone usage, future container image direction.
acceptance_criteria: nix/packages/agent-container.nix exists and defines a list of Linux-appropriate CLI packages without macOS-specific items
nix/home-agent-container.nix exists and configures Home Manager for container user without /Users/adachi references or agent-skills-nix dependency
nix/nixos/agent-container.nix exists and defines a minimal NixOS system with user adachi and home-manager integration
No references to /Users/adachi or agent-skills input in any of the new Linux-targeted files
README.md has a NixOS Agent Container section with usage instructions
The Nix code is syntactically valid (proper let/in, balanced braces, correct module structure)
verification: Confirm nix/packages/agent-container.nix exists and contains expected packages (grep for key packages like codex, claude-code, nodejs_24, python313)
Confirm nix/home-agent-container.nix exists and does not contain '/Users/adachi' or 'agent-skills-nix'
Confirm nix/nixos/agent-container.nix exists with system.stateVersion, nix.settings, users.users.adachi
Confirm flake.nix still contains darwinConfigurations.macbook with its original module imports
Confirm README.md contains '## NixOS Agent Container' section
Grep all new files for '/Users/adachi' - should find zero matches
findings: Nix is not available in this sandbox so verification was structural only. All acceptance criteria validated via grep and file content checks. The agent-skills flake input remains in inputs (needed by darwin) but the new Linux outputs do not reference it. home-manager.nixosModules.home-manager is passed as a module to nixosConfigurations alongside the NixOS module.
